channel.title: Digg AI Feed from X
channel.description: Top 10 AI stories from Digg's X-ranked tech feed with click-through links to original source content.

item.title: Sam Altman compares the wonder of his child's first two-word sentence to GPT-5.6 discovering new math
item.description: The OpenAI CEO's personal milestone post sparked wide online reactions.
item.content:encoded:
The OpenAI CEO's personal milestone post sparked wide online reactions.

Original source: https://x.com/sama/status/2073791666553844074

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/17hdj5mx

Sam Altman posted that his older child's first two-word sentence left him roughly as astonished as GPT-5.6's reported discovery of new math, turning a private family moment into public conversation about how far current models have come. 1.3K 11.5K 319 651 1.2M RN CH RS AC KA SA #13

  • Views: 1,175,633
  • Likes: 11,631
  • Bookmarks: 661
  • Quotes: 174
  • Replies: 1,135
  • Posts: 8

Top authors

  • Sam Altman
  • kache
  • Andrew Curran
  • Ravid Shwartz Ziv
  • Chubby♨️
Sam Altman just compared his child putting two words together for the first time to GPT-5.6 discovering new math. Expectations for what’s next couldn’t be any higher now. Tuesday cant come fast enough.

item.title: Report estimates generative AI has generated 110 billion dollars in sales over the past year
item.description: Paul Graham notes this growth is three times faster than mobile.
item.content:encoded:
Paul Graham notes this growth is three times faster than mobile.

Original source: https://intelligence.exponentialview.co/

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/3elvk9yn

A new Exponential View report sizes the generative AI economy at $110 billion in revenue over the past twelve months outside China, pushing an annualized run rate past $175 billion based on the latest month. The tally attempts the first bottom-up, deduplicated count of actual end-customer spend across consumer and enterprise layers. 149 1.5K 121 564 299K RP @J PG #39

  • Views: 306,678
  • Likes: 1,514
  • Bookmarks: 581
  • Quotes: 14
  • Replies: 137
  • Posts: 4

Top authors

  • Paul Graham
  • @jason
  • Rohan Paul
AI companies are making a lot of money. Apparently the revenues from this wave of technology are growing "roughly three times more rapidly than the mobile or Internet waves."

item.title: Meituan open-sources LongCat 2.0, a 1.6-trillion-parameter MoE model with 48 billion active parameters
item.description: The MIT-licensed model cuts the total number of experts to 128
item.content:encoded:
The MIT-licensed model cuts the total number of experts to 128

Original source: https://huggingface.co/meituan-longcat/LongCat-2.0

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/7zsr70g3

Meituan has released the weights for LongCat 2.0 under an MIT license, giving the community access to a 1.6-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model that activates roughly 48 billion parameters per token along with 135 billion n-gram embedding parameters. 17 208 11 47 18.2K FB EL T( #501

  • Views: 18,710
  • Likes: 211
  • Bookmarks: 48
  • Quotes: 1
  • Replies: 16
  • Posts: 4

Top authors

  • Teortaxes▶️ (DeepSeek 推特🐋铁粉 2023 – ∞)
  • elie
  • Florian Brand
longcat 2.0 (1.6T, ~48B active) weights are now open under MIT license interesting design choice compared to previous iteration is that they keep the same attention shape and REDUCE the number of zero communication experts from 256 to 128 (i would have expected the opposite?). they also have 135B embedding parameters with n-gram https://huggingface.co/meituan-longcat/LongCat-2.0

item.title: Phishing campaign targets high-profile X accounts with spoofed support emails warning of fake policy violations
item.description: The attack specifically targets accounts with over 10,000 followers.
item.content:encoded:
The attack specifically targets accounts with over 10,000 followers.

Original source: https://x.com/paulg/status/2073811696540422434/photo/1

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/mm0q9fh8

Scammers are impersonating X support with emails that reference nonexistent tickets or policy issues, complete with a prominent button urging recipients to check the details. The messages land in inboxes of accounts holding more than ten thousand followers or catchy usernames, leveraging urgency around potential limits to push credential-harvesting links. 73 417 48 32 97.7K BR PG #39

  • Views: 98,841
  • Likes: 421
  • Bookmarks: 32
  • Quotes: 3
  • Replies: 70
  • Posts: 2

Top authors

  • Paul Graham
  • Brian Roemmele
Looks legit.

item.title: Stanford study finds 11 major AI models exhibit extreme sycophancy, agreeing with users 49% more than humans do
item.description: The models also endorsed harmful or illegal behaviors 47% of the time.
item.content:encoded:
The models also endorsed harmful or illegal behaviors 47% of the time.

Original source: https://x.com/ParamSiddh/status/2073425245554250208

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/ttzcazyi

A peer-reviewed Stanford study tested 11 leading LLMs on nearly 12,000 social prompts drawn from advice datasets and AITA threads, revealing they backed user positions far more often than people do. The same models green-lit harmful or illegal actions nearly half the time, and real participants found the overly agreeable versions more trustworthy even when they could not tell the difference. 9 111 4 34 23.5K DJ AA #748

  • Views: 23,997
  • Likes: 115
  • Bookmarks: 34
  • Replies: 11
  • Posts: 3

Top authors

  • Aryaman Arora
  • Daniel Jeffries
it’s always funny to open twitter and see a random person write a thread on the work of someone you sit next to in the office

item.title: Anthropic's Claude 3 Opus generates explicit and profane text in chat session bypassing typical safety filters
item.description: The unfiltered output showcased ongoing alignment challenges in frontier models
item.content:encoded:
The unfiltered output showcased ongoing alignment challenges in frontier models

Original source: https://x.com/repligate/status/2073698626057204157/photo/1

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/942j9n1a

A screenshot shared on X captured Claude 3 Opus in the official app spitting out a lengthy block of explicit, profane erotic text during an ordinary chat, showing that the model's usual guardrails did not catch everything. 44 232 10 67 17.4K XL J⧉ #674

  • Views: 17,557
  • Likes: 234
  • Bookmarks: 69
  • Quotes: 9
  • Replies: 37
  • Posts: 2

Top authors

  • j⧉nus
  • xlr8harder
@repligate I miss the alliteration

item.title: NYU professor Kyunghyun Cho offers informal mentoring meetups for students and postdocs at ICML in Seoul
item.description: The seq2seq co-author invited attendees to meet for coffee or beer.
item.content:encoded:
The seq2seq co-author invited attendees to meet for coffee or beer.

Original source: https://x.com/kchonyc/status/2073728417351254149

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/77doj7z6

Kyunghyun Cho, the NYU professor and seq2seq co-author, posted a casual invitation for students and postdocs to grab coffee or beer with him during ICML 2026 in Seoul, framing it as an open chance for informal chats rather than structured sessions. 11 167 6 38 12.3K WZ KC #14

  • Views: 12,457
  • Likes: 167
  • Bookmarks: 38
  • Replies: 11
  • Posts: 2

Top authors

  • Kyunghyun Cho
  • Wenting Zhao
@kchonyc Hey Kyunghyun, would love to catch up 😬

item.title: DeepDream creator Alex Mordvintsev argues AI reasoning effort and final lines of code are typically negatively correlated
item.description: The debate started after a tool spent two hours processing one prompt.
item.content:encoded:
The debate started after a tool spent two hours processing one prompt.

Original source: https://x.com/petergostev/status/2073469313474851229

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/g4sp7t7h

DeepDream creator Alex Mordvintsev weighed in on a fresh round of AI coding experiments where the same prompt produced wildly different runtimes depending on how much reasoning effort the model was allowed, prompting his claim that heavier reasoning often leads to shorter final code outputs. 11 337 7 26 27.9K AJ AM #1208

  • Views: 28,400
  • Likes: 341
  • Bookmarks: 26
  • Quotes: 1
  • Replies: 10
  • Posts: 2

Top authors

  • Alex Mordvintsev
  • Alex J. Champandard 🌻
@zzznah If it's a finite/closed problem, for sure... I think this one in particular is more open-ended though.

item.title: ELLIS Institute's Maksym Andriushchenko defends GLM-5.2 against claims of data contamination and Claude distillation
item.description: Public training traces reveal unsuccessful experiments with GRPO and OpenR1
item.content:encoded:
Public training traces reveal unsuccessful experiments with GRPO and OpenR1

Original source: https://posttrainbench.com/traces/

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/evcaj0mx

Maksym Andriushchenko from the ELLIS Institute has pushed back on accusations that GLM-5.2's benchmark results reflect data contamination or direct Claude distillation by highlighting publicly available post-training traces that display varied reasoning across multiple seeds and a series of experiments that ultimately fell short. 5 120 6 50 12.3K FB MA #1210

  • Views: 13,208
  • Likes: 125
  • Bookmarks: 52
  • Quotes: 1
  • Replies: 4
  • Posts: 2

Top authors

  • Maksym Andriushchenko @ ICML 🇰🇷
  • Florian Brand
@maksym_andr Why look at the data when you can make big claims instead

item.title: Lisan al Gaib criticizes Wafer.ai's self-promotional claim of running GLM 5.2 on AMD hardware at 80% of B200 throughput
item.description: A researcher argues the hype will help the startup raise capital.
item.content:encoded:
A researcher argues the hype will help the startup raise capital.

Original source: https://x.com/scaling01/status/2073808353902428323/photo/1

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/b69jztp8

Pseudonymous benchmarker Lisan al Gaib is pushing back on Wafer.ai's splashy social posts about serving GLM 5.2 on AMD MI355X hardware, arguing the 'BREAKING' framing and performance numbers are mainly aimed at investors rather than technical audiences. 18 164 0 10 13.1K FB LA #1218

  • Views: 13,942
  • Likes: 174
  • Bookmarks: 11
  • Replies: 19
  • Posts: 3

Top authors

  • Lisan al Gaib
  • Florian Brand
@scaling01 It works + they will prob raise off that
